title: Bharat Coking Coal falls 8% after Q1FY27 net loss - The HinduBusinessLine
text:
Coal India arm Bharat Coking Coal shares fell 7 per cent after the company reported a net loss of ₹68.09 crore in Q1FY27, compared with a profit of ₹176.87 crore in the same quarter last year.

The stock closed at ₹34.92 on the NSE (down 6.90 per cent), hitting a low of ₹34.40 from the previous close of ₹37.51.

Revenue from operations declined 3.5 per cent to ₹3,587.27 crore in Q1FY27 from ₹3,719.59 crore in the corresponding quarter last year.
